description: Keep Slack bridge tokens out of tmux argv when restoring the client-slack session.

userStories.title: Runtime env handoff
userStories.description: As a sandbox operator, I want the client-slack restore path to pass Slack tokens through a permission-restricted runtime env file so secrets are inherited by pi without appearing in tmux argv.
acceptanceCriteria: .devcontainer/entrypoint.sh no longer builds an env SLACK_APP_TOKEN command string.
The restore path writes only the required Slack keys to a temporary mode-600 runtime env file owned by the sandbox user.
The tmux command string contains only the runtime env file path, sources and removes it inside the child shell, then runs pi with /tmp/client-slack.log logging.
userStories.notes: Implemented in this branch; verified by entrypoint test.

userStories.title: Regression coverage and docs
userStories.description: As a future maintainer, I want tests and durable docs to capture the secret-handling invariant.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/__tests__/entrypoint.test.ts proves fixture token values are absent from the tmux command string.
The test proves pi receives the original Slack values and malicious-looking fixture content is not evaluated.
wiki/sandbox-auth-volumes.md documents the runtime env handoff and wiki/README.md stays indexed.
userStories.notes: Implemented in this branch; verified by entrypoint test and wiki-readme-index probe.
